channel.title: The Calculator App Blog
channel.description: Financial guides, mortgage help, tax explained and savings strategy for UK and US readers.

item.title: How Much Do I Need to Retire in the UK?
item.description: A moderate retirement costs £31,300/year. With the State Pension offset, you need around £495,000. Here is how to calculate your exact target.

item.title: What Is an ISA? Types, Allowances and Which One to Use
item.description: 20,000 per year sheltered from tax. Cash ISA, Stocks and Shares ISA, and Lifetime ISA explained with exactly which is right for your goal.

item.title: How Much Should I Save Each Month in the UK?
item.description: The benchmark is 20% of take-home pay. Here is the priority order, the numbers for UK salaries, and why starting now beats starting bigger later.

item.title: What Is a Good Salary in the UK in 2025?
item.description: The UK median salary is 37,000. Above 50,270 you are in the top 25% of earners. Here is what different salaries actually give you after tax.

item.title: How Long Does It Take to Pay Off a Mortgage?
item.description: The standard UK mortgage term is 25 years. Overpaying 200 per month cuts 5 years and saves 26,000. Here is the full breakdown.

item.title: National Insurance 2025/26 - Rates, Thresholds and What You Actually Pay
item.description: NI rates for 2025/26: 8% up to 50,270, 2% above. How NI is calculated, what it means for your take-home, and what it pays for.

item.title: What Is the Personal Allowance and Who Loses It?
item.description: The UK personal allowance is 12,570 in 2025/26. Earn over 100,000 and it starts disappearing, creating an effective 60% tax rate.

item.title: UK Income Tax Brackets 2025/26 - Exactly How Much You Keep
item.description: Frozen thresholds mean more people are paying 40% tax. Here is how the bands work and how to see your real take-home.

item.title: First-Time Buyer UK 2025 - The Numbers You Need Before You Apply
item.description: Deposit, affordability, stamp duty relief, LISAs and schemes. Everything a first-time buyer needs in 2025 with the actual numbers.

item.title: Stamp Duty UK 2025 - What You Actually Pay and When
item.description: Stamp duty rates changed in April 2025. First-time buyer thresholds dropped. Here is exactly what you owe and how it is calculated.

item.title: What Happens to Your Mortgage When Interest Rates Rise?
item.description: Rising rates hit tracker and variable mortgages immediately. Fixed rates protect you until the fix ends. Here is what rate rises mean for your payment.

item.title: How to Pay Off Your Mortgage Early - The Numbers That Actually Work
item.description: You do not need to overpay by thousands to cut years off your mortgage. Small consistent overpayments compound dramatically.

item.title: What Is LTV and Why Does It Determine Your Mortgage Rate?
item.description: Loan-to-value ratio is the single most important number in your mortgage application. Here is what it is, how it is calculated, and how to improve it.

item.title: Fixed vs Tracker Mortgage - Which Is Right for You?
item.description: Fixed gives certainty. Tracker gives flexibility. The right choice depends on your situation, risk tolerance, and what rates are doing.

item.title: How Much Can I Borrow for a Mortgage in the UK?
item.description: Lenders use income multiples, stress tests, and affordability checks. Here is exactly how they calculate your maximum mortgage.

item.title: You are Losing $147,000 by Investing Monthly Instead of Weekly
item.description: Same investment. Same market. One small change in frequency can cost you $147,000 over 30 years. Here is the math and how to fix it.

item.title: The Mortgage Cheat Code: How Weekly Payments Can Save You Years
item.description: Discover how switching to weekly mortgage payments can shave years off your mortgage and save thousands in interest.
